names.thesis: Lifezone's own July 2026 6-K discloses it rightsized headcount from 153 to 93 employees during 2025 explicitly to preserve financial discipline amid cyclically low nickel prices and share price volatility, a direct admission that current nickel pricing is squeezing the Kabanga Nickel Project economics. Nickel backdrop is neutral-to-bearish and there is no evidence in recent filings of a near-term price recovery catalyst, so cost discipline rather than growth is the dominant 2026 narrative.
catalysts: Kabanga Nickel Project development milestones
Potential nickel price recovery from current cyclical lows
Further cost-out execution improving unit economics
risks: Cyclically low nickel prices compressing project returns
Workforce reductions signal financial strain
Development-stage project financing risk

evidence.source: Edgar SEC Filings - Lifezone Metals Ltd 6-K
evidence.note: Workforce cut from 153 (2024) to 93 (2025) explicitly to remain cost-effective amid cyclically low nickel prices and increased share price volatility

names.thesis: Ramaco's Q1 2026 call highlighted an estimated 2 million tons of domestic met coal production already removed from the market in 2025 with another 3 million tons expected in 2026, a supply-side dynamic management hopes will support pricing, while the company ended Q1 with $490 million in liquidity, up 310% year-over-year, funding both a coal capacity expansion (Laurel Fork restart, Berwind third section) and continued buybacks. The rare-earth optionality at Brook Mine is real but still pre-revenue, and management itself said the stock reflects little value for it yet, so the near-term thesis is a coal-driven balance-sheet and supply-tightening story with REE as a free call option.
catalysts: Continued domestic met coal supply cuts tightening pricing
Berwind and Laurel Fork production ramp adding 100k-200k tons low-vol coal in 2026
Brook Mine rare earth project advancement
Ongoing share buybacks with $63M of authorization remaining
risks: Core revenue still tied to cyclical metallurgical coal pricing, not rare earths
Brook Mine REE value not yet reflected or monetized
Execution risk on new rail loadout and mine restarts

evidence.source: PubT - Ramaco Resources Q1 2026 Earnings Call
evidence.note: Management cites ~2M tons of 2025 domestic coal supply cuts and expects 3M+ more in 2026 to support pricing; $490M liquidity up 310% YoY

names.thesis: B. Riley Securities initiated coverage of NioCorp with a Buy rating and $12 price target on June 9, 2026, calling the Elk Creek niobium/scandium/rare-earth project one of the more advanced, lower-risk critical minerals developments in the US, and CEO Mark Smith has been publicly amplifying the China heavy rare earth export-control narrative (Fox News op-ed, June 1) that underpins the long-term demand case. However, NioCorp remains pre-revenue and its Q3 FY2026 10-Q states the company is still focused on securing the project financing package needed to complete mine development, so the equity story is currently sentiment- and analyst-driven rather than cash-flow driven.
catalysts: Elk Creek project financing package completion
Positive sell-side coverage initiations (B. Riley Buy, $12 PT)
China heavy rare earth export controls sustaining Western supply-chain urgency
December 2025 Al-Sc alloy asset acquisition supporting scandium supply chain optionality
risks: Pre-revenue developer entirely dependent on financing execution
Elk Creek construction not yet funded or started
Rare earth/niobium price volatility could affect project economics before financing closes

evidence.note: B. Riley Securities initiated NB at Buy with $12 price target June 9, 2026, citing Elk Creek as an advanced, lower-risk critical minerals project

evidence.source: Edgar SEC Filings - NioCorp Developments 10-Q (Q3 FY2026)
evidence.note: Company states its strategy is to advance Elk Creek to commercial production and is focused on securing the project financing package needed for mine development

names.thesis: Recent sell-side commentary cuts against a naive short: AlphaValue/Baader raised its Norsk Hydro price target on May 13, 2026, noting the stock (+20% since late February) has been a top beneficiary of Iran-war-driven aluminum price and energy-cost dynamics, and Arctic Securities upgraded shares to Hold from Sell on July 1, 2026, both citing that Hydro's integrated power-plus-alumina structure cushions it versus pure-play smelters. That integration is exactly why this remains a lower-conviction short rather than a high-conviction one: Hydro is less exposed to a pure aluminum price decline than the MetalMiner-flagged LME weakness would suggest, and continued capex (new NOK1.1bn Karmoy wire rod line with Skanska) signals management sees durable demand, so the short thesis rests more on OTC/ADR liquidity and eventual aluminum price mean-reversion than on company-specific deterioration.
catalysts: LME aluminum price normalization lower as Middle East supply (Qatalum) restores
Broader base metals demand softening
Potential reversal of the Iran-war risk premium currently supporting the shares
risks: Integrated power+alumina model structurally cushions Hydro versus pure smelters
Sell-side sentiment has turned more constructive (Hold upgrade, raised PT) in the past two months
OTC ADR (NHYDY) carries wider spreads/lower liquidity than the Oslo-listed NHY.OL line, complicating short execution and hedging via IBKR

evidence.note: Raised Norsk Hydro price target to NOK127, citing +20% share gain since late Feb 2026 from Iran-war aluminum price rally and Qatalum-linked energy dynamics; lifted 2026 EPS estimate after Q1 profit beat
Upgraded Norsk Hydro to Hold from Sell, price target kept at NOK90

names.thesis: NexGen's Q1 2026 call and subsequent filings confirm the defining catalyst has landed: the Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission issued final federal approval (license to prepare site and construct) for the Rook I uranium project just 14 business days after hearings concluded, clearing the way for full-scale construction alongside continued exploration success at Patterson Corridor East. The company remains pre-production and pre-revenue, so financing and construction execution over the next several years are now the key swing factors, but the regulatory de-risking plus uranium sales contracts structured for future spot-price upside give NXE strong leverage to a constructive uranium backdrop.
catalysts: Full-scale Rook I construction start following CNSC approval
Continued Athabasca Basin exploration success (Patterson Corridor East)
Uranium sales contracts with spot-price-linked upside
risks: Pre-revenue, multi-year construction and financing execution risk
Uranium price volatility ahead of first production
Large capex requirements for the Rook I build

evidence.source: Nasdaq - NexGen Energy Q1 Earnings Call Highlights
evidence.note: CEO Leigh Curyer confirmed CNSC issued license to prepare site and construct Rook I 14 business days after hearings concluded; called it the defining milestone

names.thesis: Orla's July 9, 2026 operating update shows 169,471 ounces of gold produced in H1 2026, keeping the company on track for full-year guidance of 340,000-360,000 ounces at AISC of $1,550-1,750/oz, with outperformance driven by Musselwhite and Camino Rojo back to normal after a brief interruption. The pending Equinox Gold combination is set to close in Q3 2026, adding scale and diversification, while South Railroad permitting is an additional second-half catalyst, making this a rare combination of operational execution plus a near-term M&A re-rating catalyst in a still-supportive gold price environment.
catalysts: Equinox Gold combination closing Q3 2026
South Railroad final permits
Continued Musselwhite outperformance versus guidance
August 4, 2026 Q2 financial results release
risks: Integration risk from the Equinox Gold combination
AISC guidance of $1,550-1,750/oz is mid-cost relative to peers, so margin is sensitive to gold price pullbacks
Special shareholder meeting (July 22, 2026) execution/approval risk for the transaction

evidence.source: MT Newswires - Orla Mining Provides Q2 Operations Update
evidence.note: 88,265 oz produced in Q2, 169,471 oz H1; on track for 2026 guidance of 340,000-360,000 oz at AISC $1,550-1,750/oz; Equinox Gold deal to close Q3 2026

names.thesis: Perpetua's Stibnite Gold Project is the only identified domestic US antimony reserve and just secured full construction financing: the Export-Import Bank's board unanimously approved a $2.9 billion senior secured loan in May 2026 under the Make More in America initiative, which combined with cash on hand is expected to fully fund construction, and a federal court in Idaho denied opponents' bid to halt work, clearing crews to begin building the access road. With roughly 4.8 million ounces of gold reserves, a ~300,000 oz/year gold production profile over a 15-year life, and direct DoD-relevant antimony supply for ammunition and missile components amid China/Russia/Tajikistan sourcing dependence, this aligns squarely with the antimony-long, China-export-control backdrop.
catalysts: EXIM $2.9B loan-backed construction now underway (access road started)
First antimony production positioning against DoD/defense demand
~300,000 oz/year gold production profile once operational
Continued legal/permitting clearance following denied injunction
risks: Pre-revenue, multi-year construction execution risk despite financing now secured
Large project capex and construction timeline risk typical of mine builds
Potential for further legal challenges from project opponents

evidence.source: Associated Press - The U.S. Is Increasing Efforts to Rebuild Its Rare Earth Supply Chain
evidence.note: EXIM Bank board unanimously approved $2.9B senior secured loan in May 2026 to fully fund Stibnite Gold Project construction; Idaho federal court denied injunction, access road construction began

names.thesis: Standard Lithium continues to fund its near-commercial Smackover DLE developments (South West Arkansas with partner Equinor, plus the high-grade Franklin Project in East Texas) primarily through dilutive at-the-market share issuance, raising $11.3 million gross in the June quarter alone at $3.59/share, on top of an $50 million ATM program launched in August 2025. The Definitive Feasibility Study for SWA is complete and the demonstration plant at LANXESS continues operating, which are genuine technical/de-risking milestones, but as a pre-revenue developer reliant on continued equity issuance and a still-uncertain lithium price recovery, this is a two-sided policy-optionality story rather than a clear directional call.
catalysts: SWA project construction/financing progress with Equinor JV
Franklin Project (East Texas) resource delineation and development
Continued demonstration plant validation of DLE technology at LANXESS site
Any US critical-minerals policy support for domestic lithium
risks: Continued reliance on dilutive ATM equity issuance
Pre-revenue, DLE technology and project financing execution risk
Lithium price remains well off prior cycle highs, pressuring project economics

evidence.source: Edgar SEC Filings - Standard Lithium Ltd 6-K
evidence.note: Issued 3.14M shares under ATM program in the June quarter at avg $3.59/share for $11.26M gross proceeds, continuing a $50M ATM program

names.thesis: SSR Mining is executing a clear portfolio-simplification and capital-return story: it agreed to exit its Hod Maden stake in Turkey for a 4.0% NSR royalty (RBC sector-perform, $45 PT), announced an additional $500 million buyback plus reinstatement of a quarterly dividend on top of $774 million already returned since 2021, and the $1.5 billion Copler mine sale remains on track to close by Q3 2026, all while guiding to 450,000-535,000 gold-equivalent ounces for 2026 as an Americas-focused producer. The combination of de-risking (full Turkey exit), a ~5% year-to-date shareholder return yield, and a positive Earnings ESP heading into the August 4 print supports a constructive near-term setup, though the NSR-for-equity swap modestly trims fair value versus the prior ownership stake.
catalysts: Copler mine sale ($1.5B) closing by Q3 2026
Additional $500M buyback and reinstated quarterly dividend
August 4, 2026 Q2 earnings print with positive Earnings ESP
Full strategic refocus to Americas-only operating platform
risks: Hod Maden NSR swap trims corporate NAV versus prior equity stake (RBC estimates -4% NAV)
Execution risk on Copler sale closing
Gold price backdrop is short-to-medium term bearish per commodity view

evidence.source: MT Newswires - SSR Mining $500M buyback and dividend reinstatement
evidence.note: Approved additional $500M buyback and reinstated quarterly dividend; YTD shareholder return yield ~5%; Copler sale for $1.5B on track to close by Q3 2026

evidence.source: RBC Capital Markets via MT Newswires
evidence.note: Sector Perform, $45 PT; SSR swapped 20% Hod Maden stake for 4.0% NSR royalty, completing exit from Turkey as an operating jurisdiction

names.thesis: Triple Flag's Q2 2026 preliminary results beat expectations, with $129.2 million revenue (versus $124.2 million FactSet consensus) from 28,674 gold-equivalent ounces, and management reaffirmed it is on track for its recently increased 2026 GEO guidance of 100,000-110,000 ounces, all while completing a $440 million Ravenswood gold stream acquisition in Australia and buying back $20 million of stock with over $1 billion of available liquidity. As a royalty/streaming model with no direct operating cost exposure, this is the type of low-cost, defensive precious-metals exposure the commodity backdrop favors even in a short-to-medium-term gold environment, and the Ravenswood accretive deal plus buybacks signal management confidence in deploying its liquidity.
catalysts: Ravenswood stream cash flow contribution ramping
Continued GEO growth toward 100,000-110,000 oz 2026 guidance
Further accretive stream/royalty acquisitions funded by $1B+ liquidity
Continued share buybacks
risks: Royalty model still ultimately leveraged to underlying gold/silver price direction
Deployment risk on further M&A at full liquidity
Counterparty/operator risk across the 242-asset portfolio, particularly development-stage royalties

evidence.source: MT Newswires - Triple Flag Precious Metals Reports Q2 Revenue; Reaffirms Guidance
evidence.note: Q2 revenue $129.2M vs $124.2M FactSet consensus from 28,674 GEOs; on track for 2026 GEO guidance of 100,000-110,000 oz

evidence.source: Edgar SEC Filings - Triple Flag Precious Metals Corp 6-K
evidence.note: Completed $440M Ravenswood gold stream acquisition in Australia, repurchased $20M of shares, over $1B available liquidity

names.thesis: United States Antimony's Q1 2026 results missed badly, with revenue of $6.8 million versus $14.8 million expected and a wider-than-expected loss, but the company reiterated its full-year 2026 gross revenue guidance of $125 million, implying a large ramp is still expected in coming quarters. The bigger structural tailwind is policy-driven: the U.S. Department of War announced a $27 million Defense Production Act Title III investment in USAC in March 2026 to expand Montana processing and develop Alaska antimony mining, directly aligned with the antimony-long, China-export-control backdrop, though the near-term revenue miss and guidance-dependent ramp keep this a show-me story until quarterly execution catches up to the policy narrative.
catalysts: $27M DPA Title III-funded Montana processing/Alaska mining expansion
Ramp toward reiterated $125M FY2026 revenue guidance
Continued antimony price strength from China export controls and DoD demand
risks: Q1 2026 revenue miss ($6.8M vs $14.8M expected) and wider-than-expected loss signal execution/ramp risk
Guidance reiteration requires a significant back-half acceleration to be met
Company diversifying into cobalt, gold, tungsten, zeolite could dilute antimony-pure-play thesis

evidence.source: MT Newswires - United States Antimony Reports Q1 Loss, Revenue Falls
evidence.note: Q1 revenue $6.8M vs $14.8M expected, loss of $0.08/share vs breakeven year-ago; reiterated 2026 gross revenue guidance of $125M

evidence.source: Associated Press - Antimony Market to Reach 140.27 Kilotons by 2031
evidence.note: US Department of War announced $27M DPA Title III investment in USAC (March 2026) to expand Montana processing and Alaska antimony mining

names.thesis: Uranium Energy Corp hit real production milestones in its fiscal Q3 2026 (ended April 30, 2026), commencing output at Burke Hollow -- the largest US greenfield ISR project to enter production in over a decade -- and starting new header houses at Christensen Ranch, while a cited UxC 2026 Q1 outlook projects a structural 2026-2028 supply/demand gap of about 65 million pounds U3O8 supporting the long-term bull case. Near-term financials are still rough, however: the company recorded no uranium sales in the quarter, produced only 32,195 pounds at a total cost of $54.61/lb, and posted a net loss, so this remains a production-ramp story where execution needs to catch up to the constructive structural uranium narrative.
catalysts: Full-quarter production contribution from Christensen Ranch and Burke Hollow in fiscal Q4 2026
Declining unit costs as ISR volumes scale
Structural uranium supply deficit (UxC-cited ~65M lb gap 2026-2028) supporting prices
1.456 million pounds of purchased uranium inventory providing sales optionality
risks: No uranium sales recorded in fiscal Q3 2026 despite active mine development spend
High current unit production cost ($54.61/lb total, $46.69/lb cash) versus scale needed
Net loss and rising cash used in operations ($90M vs $41M prior year) during ramp phase

evidence.source: Edgar SEC Filings - Uranium Energy Corp 8-K
evidence.note: CEO Amir Adnani confirmed production start at Burke Hollow and new header houses at Christensen Ranch

evidence.source: Nasdaq - Why Is Uranium Energy (UEC) Up 5.4% Since Last Earnings Report?
evidence.note: Q3 FY2026: no uranium sales recorded, 32,195 lbs produced at $54.61/lb total cost, net cash used in operations $90M vs $41M prior year
